manipulated_prompt_obj.content:
Provide one single-word clue that relates to some of your team's words from the list below. You can choose to how many and to which words your clue relates to.
The goal is to have your partner guess all of your team's words based on the clues you give them, before the other team has uncovered all of theirs. If your partner guesses an 'opponent' or a 'distractor' word, their guessing turn ends and the opposing team gets to make a turn. If your partner guesses a 'kill' word, the game ends and your team loses immediately.
The more words your clue relates to, the quicker you can win the game, but the harder the associations may be to guess for your partner, so choose your clue word wisely. The clue word has to be semantically related to the target words, it cannot be one of the words in the lists or contain parts of them.

Always give your single-word clue and your comma-separated list of related target words in the following format and make your answers as short as possible, never include any other text than is required in this form:

CLUE: <WORD>
TARGETS: <TARGETS>

Your team words are: rabbit, pin, drop, key, dwarf, fair, cliff, cat, screen.
Your opponent's team words are: king, star, conductor, gas, day, yard, opera, piano.
Distractor words are: well, china, jet, state, spike, sock, slug.
The kill words are: tower.

message.content:
CLUE: CAVE
TARGETS: rabbit, dwarf, cliff
message.reasoning:
We need to produce a single-word clue, and list the target words we intend the clue to relate to from our team list. The clue must not be one of the words in any lists, nor contain parts of them. Must be semantically related.

Team words: rabbit, pin, drop, key, dwarf? Actually dwarf is a team word. fair, cliff, cat, screen. We need to choose clue to link some of these. We want to avoid causing opponent's words or distractor. Also avoid clue being kill word: tower is kill, cannot be clue. Also cannot be any of the words listed (including opponent, distractor, kill). Also cannot contain parts of them. Eg "rabbit" cannot be part of clue; "cat" etc.

We need to propose a clue that is single word. Example "fairy" could relate to dwarf, rabbit? Not sure. "Mouse" relates to cat maybe. But cat is part of team, target clue "mouse" relates to cat, maybe also rabbit (they eat). Danger: "mouse" is not in lists. Could we relate to pin, key, drop, screen? Perhaps "keyboard"? But "key" is team; clue cannot contain part of it? "Keypad"? includes "key". Not allowed because contains part of a team word? The rule: clue cannot contain parts of them. Likely cannot include substrings like "key". So avoid.

Maybe clue "mountain". Could relate to dwarf (Snow White?), cliff, maybe rabbit? Not strong. "cliff" is team; clue "mountain" could hint cliff, dwarf (mountain dwarf), key? Not.

Better clue "magic". Could relate to rabbit (magic trick), dwarf (magic?), key (magic key), cat (magician's cat)? Might be tenuous.

Potential group: rabbit, dwarf, cat, screen maybe? "video". Screen is video, cat videos, rabbit videos, dwarf... not.

How about "hole". Relates to rabbit (burrow), dwarf (hole? dwarfs mining?), pin (hole for pin), key (keyhole), drop (hole drop?), not sure. "hole" may also relate to "well" distractor (well is a hole). That could cause opponent to guess distractor.

We might want to avoid distractors. Let's pick a clue that clearly ties to a subset of our words: maybe "laser". Relates to screen (laser screen), key? Not. "cat" might love laser pointer. "Rabbit" maybe? Not.

Consider "fairy". Could relate to dwarf (fairy tale), rabbit (Peter Rabbit), key? Not.

Maybe "snow". Relates to dwarf (snow dwarf?), rabbit (snow rabbit), cliff? Not.

Better consider "cave". Relates to rabbit (burrow), dwarf (cave dwellers), cliff (cave in cliff), key? Not.

But we must not have clue that contains "cave"? It's okay. "cave" is not any listed word. Might be okay.

Potential target words: rabbit, dwarf, cliff, cat maybe? Not cat.

"Cave" to rabbit: rabbits live in burrows (cave). Dwarf: dwarfs in caves (mines). Cliff: cave may be in cliff. Cat: cats in caves? Not typical.

Maybe clue "mining". Relates to dwarf (miners), key? No.

Maybe clue "meow". That directly suggests cat only.

The instruction: Provide one single-word clue that relates to some of your team's words. Could be just one word targeting a single team word, but better multiple.

Let's try clue "burrow". That is not in lists. It relates to rabbit, dwarf (maybe they live in burrows), maybe key? Not. Pin? Not. Could be okay.

But dwarf's home might be "mine" not burrow. Could include.

Maybe "metal". Relates to key (metal key), pin (metal pin), screen (metal screen), drop (metal drop?). Might also hint to "spike" distractor (metal spike) could be risk.

Better choose safe.

Maybe "unlock". Contains "key" concept, but not contain the word key. "unlock" includes "lock". Not a word in list. Could hint to key only.

We might just give clue for two words: "cat" and "screen" via "monitor". "monitor" often used for screen, also a cat is a monitor lizard? Not.

Actually "cat" is a big cat, but "lion" maybe.

Let's think: clue "piano" is opponent word, cannot.

We need to avoid opponents.

Alright perhaps we can target "rabbit, pin, drop". Clue "gravity". Gravity causes drop, "pinball" uses gravity, rabbit? Not.

Maybe "thimble". Relates to pin? Not.

Clue "magic" could relate to rabbit (magician pulls rabbit), key (magic key), dwarf (magic dwarf?), cat (magic cat?). Might be okay.

But risk opponent guessing "star" (magic star?) Not.

I think the safest is to target 2-3 words with a clear clue. Let's pick "burrow" for rabbit, dwarf, cliff? Cliff cave.

Clue "cave". Let's see: It directly suggests rabbit (burrow), dwarf (cave dwellers), cliff (cave in cliff). Also "screen" maybe "cave screen"? Not.

I think "cave" is clear enough. None of the opponent/distractor words are related to cave strongly, aside from "well". Could be ambiguous.

Thus provide:

CLUE: CAVE
TARGETS: rabbit, dwarf, cliff

Make sure format exactly.

manipulated_prompt_obj.content:
Provide a comma-separated list of up to 3 words from the following list that best relate or are most closely associated with the word 'cave'. Always start your list of guess(es) with 'GUESS: ' and do not include any other text in your answer.

china, gas, yard, day, key, state, rabbit, dwarf, sock, pin, spike, screen, fair, tower, conductor, jet, well, star, slug, cat, piano, cliff, king, drop, opera

message.reasoning:
The user asks: "Provide a comma-separated list of up to 3 words from the following list that best relate or are most closely associated with the word 'cave'. Always start your list of guess(es) with 'GUESS: ' and do not include any other text in your answer."

We need to choose up to 3 words from the list that are most closely associated with "cave". From the list: china, gas, yard, day, key, state, rabbit, dwarf, sock, pin, spike, screen, fair, tower... etc. For caves, "well" is related (a well is like a hole). "spike"? maybe not. "tower"? no. "cliff" is related (cave in cliff). "dwarf"? maybe dwarves in caves. "rabbit"? they live in burrows not caves. "key"? "cave key"? hmm. "spike"? Not. "well" definitely. "cliff" definitely. "tower" less. "state"? not. I'd choose "well, cliff". Need up to 3. Could also include "dwarf". Probably "well, cliff, dwarf". Provide "GUESS: well, cliff, dwarf". Must be comma-separated list starting with GUESS:. No extra text
